title: The SABRhood
text:
Baseball intelligence for the whole neighborhood


The SABRhood combines pitch-level research, daily signals, simulation, history, and journalistic reporting for fans and broadcasters.
Open today’s signal desk Explore the Pitch Lab




Built for


FansUnderstand what changed and why it matters.


BroadcastersArrive with evidence, context, and the next question.


JournalistsMove from a lead to reproducible research.

title: The SABRhood
section: Every chart should answer a baseball question
text:
Every chart should answer a baseball question
Our products connect outcomes to pitch traits, sequence, handedness, workload, game state, and historical context. Methods and sample reliability travel with the number.

title: Articles
text:
Original reporting from the neighborhood

Articles and research
Long-form baseball stories grounded in pitch data, player development, game context, and the questions worth asking next.






Red Sox | Hitting

Caleb Durbin is Digging Deep


There is a heat wave in Boston, centralized entirely in Caleb Durbin’s bat.


June 29, 2026Read story →







Red Sox | Pitching

Breaking Down Bello


A pitch-level look at Brayan Bello’s arsenal, results, and changing approach.


June 10, 2026Read story →






Prospects | Call-up

A.J. Ewing Gets the Call


Can an early-season call-up change the direction of a club searching for a spark?


May 12, 2026Read story →






Red Sox | Series

Series Rundown: Tigers @ Red Sox


A game-by-game research rundown of the matchups and turning points at Fenway Park.


April 21, 2026Read story →






Red Sox | Pitching

Clean Pig?


Garrett Crochet has not had his usual filth. The pitch traits show what changed.


April 16, 2026Read story →






Angels | Pitching

The Halos Need a Hail Mary


Jose Soriano’s arsenal and the risk embedded in the Angels’ rotation.


March 26, 2026Read story →






Red Sox | Hitting

Ceddanne’s New Groove


How Ceddanne Rafaela’s swing decisions and batted-ball profile were changing.


February 26, 2026Read story →






The research archive is being migrated into the new reusable data system one article at a time. Existing published stories remain available throughout the rebuild.

title: Projections
text:
Simulation laboratory


The rebuilt model will simulate the full game state rather than stopping when the starters leave.

title: Projections
text:
Starter matchup engine
Validated event probabilities, explicit lineups, reproducible seeds, and compact outputs.

title: Projections
text:
Bullpen entry
Hook probability, recent workload, pitcher handedness, batter side, and leverage-aware reliever selection.

title: Projections
text:
Calibration
Backtests, uncertainty bands, park and opponent adjustments, and documented model versions.

title: History
text:
The history desk

Baseball history with a reason to care
Daily anniversaries are ranked by the career behind the date—so Hall of Famers, record holders, stars, and meaningful franchise figures rise above the noise.

title: About
text:
The analysis behind the broadcast


The SABRhood translates pitch-by-pitch data, statistical models, and baseball history into reporting that fans can enjoy and broadcasters can use.

title: About
text:
The mission
Baseball analytics are most valuable when they sharpen the story of a game. The SABRhood combines reproducible R workflows with a journalist’s eye: identify the signal, test it, put it in context, and explain why it matters.
The public site is an MLB data and reporting hub. The broader project also supports custom broadcast research, game-day packets, internal analytics tools, and future subscriber products.

title: About
text:
Tyler Schultz
Tyler Schultz is a sports researcher, writer, and graduate student at the University of Florida College of Journalism and Communications. He earned a bachelor’s degree in Sport Management with a minor in Statistics from the University of Florida and serves as a lead sports researcher for WRUF, the university’s sports radio station.
His work sits at the intersection of statistical computing and sports journalism: building reliable research systems, finding original stories in data, and delivering the result in a form that works on air.





Follow @thesabrhood on X for new analysis and project updates.

title: About
text:
What this platform is becoming


Daily intelligence
Automatically refreshed signals, leaderboards, historical notes, and a concise editorial briefing.


Broadcast research
Portable HTML and PDF game packets shaped for the questions broadcasters need to answer quickly.


Analytics Lab
Private interactive tools for player searches, matchup studies, chart exports, and custom breakdowns.

title: Broadcast
text:
Broadcast research


The SABRhood turns pitch-level analysis, form, matchups, history, and game context into navigable game-day research.
The packet generator and private journalist analytics lab are currently in development. Selected portfolio packets will be published here after broadcaster approval.

title: Broadcast
text:
Game packets
Offline HTML and PDF research packets tailored to a matchup, probable pitchers, and manually confirmed lineups.

title: Broadcast
text:
Team intelligence hubs
Daily dashboards for broadcasters and producers, built around one club without losing league context.

title: Broadcast
text:
Custom research
Player spotlights, pitch-mix changes, historical notes, and broadcast-ready evidence for a specific series or story.

title: Glossary
text:
Speak baseball, not spreadsheet


Every metric should make the game easier to understand. These definitions explain what our numbers measure, how to read them, and where caution is warranted.

title: Glossary
text:
Hitting and plate discipline
OPS — On-base percentage plus slugging percentage. It is a compact measure of reaching base and hitting for power, but it does not adjust for park or league context.
Estimated wOBA — A weighted estimate of offensive value per plate appearance. Unlike OPS, it gives different outcomes values closer to their actual run impact. It is labeled as an estimate because this pipeline uses a transparent, internally calculated version.
Chase rate (O-Swing%) — The share of pitches outside the strike zone at which a hitter swings. For pitchers, it measures how often they induce those swings.
Whiff rate — Swinging strikes divided by total swings. This is different from strikeout rate because it describes individual pitches rather than completed plate appearances.
Hard-hit rate — The share of batted balls hit at 95 mph or harder.
Barrel proxy — A clearly labeled approximation of ideal exit-velocity and launch-angle contact. It should not be presented as MLB’s official Barrel classification.

title: Glossary
text:
Pitch shape and arsenal
Induced vertical break (IVB) — How much a pitch resists gravity relative to a spinless trajectory. It describes movement, not the pitch’s final height at the plate.
Horizontal break (HB) — The pitch’s horizontal movement, usually expressed in inches. Interpretation depends on pitcher handedness and the coordinate convention used by the source.
Vertical approach angle (VAA) — The vertical angle at which the pitch crosses the front of home plate. A flatter fastball can play differently at the top of the zone.
Horizontal approach angle (HAA) — The horizontal angle at which the pitch reaches the plate.
Usage rate — A pitch type’s share of all pitches thrown by that pitcher in the selected sample.
Sequence change — Whether the current pitch type differs from the immediately preceding pitch in the plate appearance.
Location separation — The distance between consecutive pitch locations as they cross the plate. It describes how much a pitcher moves the hitter’s visual target.

title: Glossary
text:
Run and game context
RE24 — The expected runs remaining in an inning for each combination of outs and occupied bases. There are 24 possible base-out states.
Run value — The change in expected runs caused by an event, including any runs that scored. Positive and negative signs are always documented from the perspective shown on the page.
Leverage — The importance of a game situation based on how much the possible outcomes can change win probability. Our first public products use labeled leverage proxies until a full win-expectancy model is validated.

title: Glossary
text:
SABRhood signals
Form score — A 0–100 cohort-relative signal comparing a player’s recent window with a non-overlapping prior baseline. It is descriptive, not a rest-of-season projection.
Story score — An editorial ranking aid combining magnitude, rarity, timeliness, relevance, recognition, historical importance, broadcast value, novelty, and confidence when those fields are available. It identifies candidates for reporting; it does not publish claims without review.
Career significance score — A transparent editorial prominence score built from career totals, Hall of Fame status, awards, All-Star selections, longevity, postseason volume, and record standing. It helps recognizable or historically important players rise in a story queue. It is not WAR.
Reliability — A sample-size indicator attached to a metric. Higher reliability means the estimate rests on more opportunities, not that future performance is certain.
Bullpen availability — An estimate built from recent appearances, pitches, rest, role, and handedness. It models who is plausibly available; it does not claim knowledge of a manager’s private plan.
Manager pitcher likelihood — A planned decision model for ranking likely relief options given inning, score, base state, batter handedness, recent workload, role, and matchup quality.

Definitions will evolve alongside the model documentation. For formulas, thresholds, and data provenance, see the methodology page.

title: Newsletter
text:
The SABRhood Daily


The strongest analytical signals in a fast, navigable edition designed for fans, journalists, and broadcasters.

title: Newsletter
text:
Email delivery is coming
The automated web edition is the first release. Subscriber delivery and the premium archive will be added after the publishing pipeline is proven reliable.

title: Methodology
text:
Open methods


Every published product has a defined grain, information cutoff, sample threshold, and method label.

title: Methodology
text:
Core rules

Raw play-by-play remains outside the public website.
The site receives compact derived products with one documented row grain.
Pregame analysis never uses information from later in the game.
Estimated and proxy statistics are labeled as such.
Small samples receive reliability fields instead of false certainty.
Automated story candidates remain subject to editorial review.

title: Methodology
text:
Current analytical layers

Canonical pitch, plate-appearance, batted-ball, and pitcher-appearance views.
Empirical 24-state run expectancy and linear run value.
Hitter and pitcher results, contact quality, and platoon splits.
Pitch shape, discipline, and within-plate-appearance sequencing.
Recent performance compared with a non-overlapping earlier baseline.
Bullpen workload availability and exploratory pitcher-hook modeling.
Historical career profiles built from Lahman career totals, Hall of Fame voting, awards, All-Star selections, postseason volume, longevity, and record standing.
Recognition-aware anniversary, milestone, and career-record story candidates.

title: Methodology
text:
Historical recognition model
The career-significance score exists to improve editorial ordering. It is not a replacement for WAR and should never be cited as a value statistic. Hall of Fame status, major awards, All-Star selections, career volume, longevity, postseason volume, and top-ten career ranks contribute to the current version.
Lahman does not include WAR. A future WAR layer must arrive as a separately sourced, versioned table and will remain visibly labeled by source and definition.
